question: An un-interruptible unit is known as ____________
Semaphore is a/an _______ to solve the critical section problem.
What are the two atomic operations permissible on semaphores?

options.a: CPU cycles wasting locks over critical sections of programs
options.b: Locks that avoid time wastage in context switches
options.c: Locks that work better on multiprocessor systems

question: What is the main disadvantage of spinlocks?

options.a: they are not sufficient for many process
options.d: they are too complex for programmers

question: The wait operation of the semaphore basically works on the basic _______ system call.
The signal operation of the semaphore basically works on the basic _______ system call.
If the semaphore value is negative ____________

options.a: its magnitude is the number of processes waiting on that semaphore
options.c: no operation can be further performed on it until the signal operation is performed on it

question: The code that changes the value of the semaphore is ____________

explanation: P0 prints once, then either P1 or P2 releases S0; the other can still release S0 later, so P0 prints at least twice.

question:
Each process Pi, i = 0,1,2,3,……,9 is coded as follows.

repeat
 P(mutex)
 {Critical Section}
 V(mutex)
 forever

---
The code for P10 is identical except that it uses V(mutex) instead of P(mutex). What is the largest number of processes that can be inside the critical section at any moment (the mutex being initialized to 1)?
explanation: One of P0..P9 can enter and set mutex to 0. P10 can then increment it to 1 and enter. Another of P0..P9 can then decrement to 0 and enter, for a maximum of 3.

options.a: It does not ensure mutual exclusion
options.b: It does not ensure bounded waiting
options.c: It requires that processes enter the critical section in strict alternation
options.d: It does not prevent deadlocks but ensures mutual exclusion
